texts.full_text: a useful parameter for predicting the histological type of thymic epithelial tumors. We assessed the utility of the tumor doubling time (TDT) for predicting the histological type of thymic epithelial tumors. We retrospectively reviewed 130 patients with thymic epithelial tumors who underwent computed tomography two or more times beforesurgery. The patients were divided into low-risk thymoma(types A, AB and B1), high-risk thymoma (types B2 and B3) and thymic carcinoma (thymic carcinomaand thymic neuroendocrine tumor) groups. In the 96 patients who showed tumor enlargement, the relationship between the histological type and the TDT of the tumor was investigated. The study population included 55 men and 41 women from 26to 82 years of age. The TDT of the thymic carcinoma group (median 205 days) was significantly shorter in comparison to the low-risk thymoma (median 607 days) and high-risk thymoma (median 459 days) groups. No ssignificant difference was found in the TDT between the low-risk thymoma and high-risk thymoma

texts.full_text: part of the thesis investigates the effect of increased pressure on the main SCR reaction, which was studied using a catalyst containing about 1 wt% V2O5/10wt%WO3/TiO2 supplied by Umicore Denmark ApS. The SCR reaction was studied across granulated catalysts (150-300 microns) in steady state experiments, for which the NOx conversion was found independent of the pressure when the residence time was kept constant. This shows that the kinetics of the SCR reaction were not affected by the increased pressure of up to 5 bar. NH3 temperature programmed desorption (TPD) experiments were also conducted for the granulated catalyst. The results showed that the adsorption of NH3 increased with pressure. The desorption proles were modeled using a Temkin isotherm (i.e.a coverage dependent desorption enthalpi). The steady state SCR experiments were also modeled, and the SCR reaction was found independent of the catalyst surface concentration of NH3, when the surface coverage was higher than 14%. This low surface coverage also explains why the SCR reaction was unaffected by the increased NH3 adsorption because the active part of the catalyst was already covered with NH3. At a marine engine, the catalyst will be used in the form of a monolith. Therefore, SCR experiments at elevated pressure using a monolith was also conducted. Using a constant residence time, the NOx conversion was found to be lowered by up to 5% points, at an increased pressure of 3 bar and above 250‰, due to increased mass transfer limitations. The change in mass transfer limitation is due to inverse proportionality between the binary diusion constant and the pressure.The second part of this thesis investigated how the oxidation of SO2 into SO3 was affected by increased pressure. SO2 oxidation is known to be slightly catalyzed by the vanadium-based SCR catalyst. Stability: Taq Polymerase tends to lose its stability and efficiency over time when exposed repeatedly to high temperatures in a thermocycler for extended periods during PCR setup stages before starting cycles which is often referred as "pre-cycle heating". texts.full_text: APK you're trying to install is compiled for a different CPU architecture than the one on the device you're trying to install it on. Here are a few steps you can follow to resolve this issue: 1. Check Target ABIs: Make sure that your APK is compiled to support the architecture of the device you're trying to install it on. Since you're targeting Android 1.6, you're likely targeting the ARM architecture. Ensure your `build.gradle` file includes the following: ```gradle android { ... defaultConfig { ... ndk { abiFilters "armeabi-v7a", "armeabi" // ARM architectures } } } ``` Note: "armeabi-v7a" is preferable over "armeabi" if your application is using ARM v7 instructions. 2. Rebuild Your Project: After adjusting your ABI settings, clean and rebuild your project. This ensures that your APK is correctly compiled for the intended architectures. ```bash ./gradlew clean ./gradlew assembleDebug ``` 3. Reinstall the APK: Once your APK is rebuilt, try installing it again. Make sure to uninstall the previous version of the app if it was partially installed. ```bash adb uninstall your.package.name adb install path/to/your/apk ``` 4. Check Build Variants: Ensure that the APK you're installing is the correct build variant. Sometimes, you might build a debug version that might have issues if it's not the final version you're aiming to install. 5. Check APK Contents: Use APKTool or a similar tool to inspect the APK to verify that it's compiled for the correct architectures. 6. texts.full_text: o Dara’s recaps, the very best thing about The Real L Word is that it is ripe for mockery. (Some scenes are just ripe, I know, but that’s a different post.) Leave it to the warped-but-funny minds of Second City to hit the nail (pun intended) on the head. After all, this is the troupe that gave us Gilda Radner, Jane Lynch, Tina Fey and Amy Sedaris. You saw a preview of Second City’s Lesbian Cougars of Los Angeles a few weeks ago in Morning Brew, complete with mullets, tweed jacketsand a healthy dose of sarcasm. They were hilarious, and I couldn't get enough of it. The Real L Word has always been known for its over-the-top drama, and I think Second City has successfully captured the essence of it in their sketch. The use of satire and irony is brilliant, as it's clear that they're poking fun at the show's cliches and melodrama. And yet, it's also clear that they're coming from a place of respect and affection for the show, rather than outright mockery. That's what makes their take on The Real L Word so effective. But what really gets me is how they're not just targeting the show's absurdity, but also the way it often uses lesbian relationships as a punchline. It's like they're saying, "Okay, we get it, you're a bunch of dramatic, over-the-top women. Can we please just have some respect for each other?" texts.full_text: establishment of different symbiotic associations, including endosymbiosis, with a wide range of microorganisms. All whiteflies have a paired bacteriome that is usually orange in color ([@evv038-B7]). It is composed by specialized cells called bacteriocytes, which always present a pleomorphic bacterium, *Candidatus* (*Ca.*) *Portiera aleyrodidarum* (hereafter *Portiera*) ([@evv038-B80]). *Portiera* is an obligate primary endosymbiont located in host-derived vesicles and displaying a typical three-membrane system with one membrane derived from the insect vacuole ([@evv038-B65]). It belongs to family Halomonadaceae and, with the endosymbionts of psyllids (*Ca. Carsonella ruddii*, hereafter *Carsonella*) and moss bugs (*Ca. Evansia muelleri*, hereafter *Evansia*), it forms a phylogenetic clade currently composed exclusively by hemipteran endosymbionts ([@evv038-B42]; [@evv038-B64]). The concservation of this symbiosis across different lineages suggests that these bacteria play essential roles in the biology and ecology of their hosts, likely contributing to nutrient provisioning, particularly by synthesizing amino acids and other nutrients lacking in the whitefly diet. This mutualistic relationship is thought to have evolved as an adaptation to the nutritional constraints imposed by a diet rich in sap but poor in certain essential compounds, allowing whiteflies to thrive on plant-based nutrition that would otherwise be insufficient for their survival and reproduction. texts.full_text: hese procedures and the skin of the patient is usually some sort of biomedical electrode. Such an electrode typically includes a conductor which must be connected electrically to the equipment, and a conductive medium adhered to or otherwise contacting skin of a patient. Among the therapeutic procedures using biomedical electrodes are transcutaneous electronic nerve stimulation (TENS) devices used for pain management; neuromuscular stimulation (NMS) used for treating conditions such as scoliosis; defibrillation electrodes to dispense electrical energy to a chest cavity of a mammalian patient to defibrillate heart beats of the patient; and dispersive electrodes to receive electrical energy dispensed into an incision made during electrosurgery. Among diagnostic procedures using biomedical electrodes are monitors of electrical output from body functions, such as electrocardiogram (ECG) for monitoring heart activity and for diagnosing heart abnormalities. For each diagnostic, therapeutic, or electrosurgical procedure, at least one biomedical electrode is applied to the patient's scalp. The quality of the signal reception or delivery depends greatly on the contact impedance between the electrode and the skin. To provide good mechanical conductivity, a conductive gel or adhesive is usually applied with the electrodes. These gels help minimize interference from external signals and reduce irritation to the patient by avoiding direct metal-to-skin contact which can cause discomfort over long periods of use. The design and material composition of biomedical electrodes vary depending on their particular application: 1. TENS Electrode: usually made with a rigid, conductive pad that adheres to the skin using an electrically-conducting gel or hydrogel layer which provides good contact for receiving electrical stimulation pulses quickly and safely over extended periods of use in pain management therapies. 2. NMS Electrode: similar design principles as TENS electrodes but often smaller, designed primarily to target muscle groups with higher currents necessary for cardiac therapy or treatment protocols like scoliosis correction through electric stimulation therapy sessions lasting many minutes at a time without causing skin inflammation. 3. Defibrillator Electrode: These are small pads placed on the chest

A: Fitango.com Fitango is a fitness and goal tracking web site that goes beyond merely logging your progress and offers guides, expert-created plans, easy tracking, and peer-based motivation to keep you moving towards your goals—fitness-related or otherwise. (lifehacker review) You can create an account using Facebook, which allows you to share your results with friends. Then you either create your own workout or you get one through their Planstore. There are also other categories to get challenged in, but here's an example from the Fitness & Sports department: For this example I picked a marathon plan, you can invite Motivators. These are friends, trainers or fellow athletes, that can offer advice, motivation and help you stay on track! You have to manually keep track of your workouts, which you can then easily publish to Facebook. You can get an overview of your workouts on your Report tab, which gives you graphs and other info. Luckily they also have an app, which means you can update your Actions from your mobile phone, rather than requiring a computer. As for any further social activity, there's the Bubble. The Bubble is a place for all users of an Actionplan to discuss their progress, challenges, and experiences. Using the Bubble is simple. Just write something in the text box and click Post. Remember -- whatever you post in the Bubble is visible to all users of that Actionplan. You can easily attach photos or include links in your Bubble posts by clicking the appropriate icons. Summary Fitango has a ton of options and since it's still a beta product, they will probably add a lot of new features in the future. One downside compared to apps like RunKeeper or Endomondo, you have to manually fill in your Actions. But in return, it has a lot more social features and let's you keep track of virtually anything! A: Have you checked out ChallengeLoop? I

Although this design allows the distributed system to perform tasks in parallel, its natural advantages are not fully utilized in conventional systems because the “remote” messages are comparatively slow. That is, when objects reside on different computer systems, the distributed system manager must send messages between those systems. These inter-system messages are sent at a much slower rate than intra-system messages. This drawback can make it computationally expensive to maintain data consistency across the distributed system. An additional drawback with conventional subject/observer systems is that the subject object controls the message transmission rate. Frequently, an observer object running on a heavily burdened system may not be able handle updates from the subject object at this rate. texts.full_text: (n = 2) had vocal cord paralysis as their primary diagnosis, with one patient also having subglottic stenosis; both underwent early unplanned tracheotomies within a week of surgery due to extubation failure and stridor. Factors contributing to the need for an unplanned tracheostomy included: prolonged intubations leading to laryngo-tracheal injury, recurrent aspiration events causing respiratory compromise (n = 3), failed multiple attempts at extubation due to upper airway obstruction or weak cough reflex and inability of the patient to handle secretions. Other notable factors were low birth weight with a median of 2 kg for preterm infants, chromosomal abnormalities such as Down syndrome (n =3), congenital heart defects requiring prolonged cardiopulmonary bypass time during surgery and postoperative complications including vocal cord paralysis. In conclusion, unplanned tracheostomy following pediatric cardiac surgeries in children under 12 months was associated with multiple factors such as underlying syndromes or chromosomal abnormalities, prolonged intubation leading to airway injury and postoperative complications. texts.full_text: , there are a few measures you can take to enhance your privacy and minimize the risk of IP address leakage. - Use a VPN Private Network): A VPN can mask your IP address and provide a layer of privacy and security. When you connect to a VPN, your device's IP address is replaced with the IP address of the VPN server, making your IP address and location virtually untraceable. - Use a Proxy Server: A proxy server can act as an intermediary, forwarding your requests to the internet and sending the responses to you. This can help to hide your IP address as the proxy server's IP address is used instead of your own. - Disable WebRTC: WebRTC is a technology used to enable connections, and it can potentially leak your IP address. You can disable WebRTC in your browser to prevent this. - Use a Privacy-Focused Browser: Consider using a browser that is designed to prioritize your privacy, such as Brave, Tor Browser, or Epic Privacy Browser. These browsers come with built-in privacy and security features, and some even disable WebRTC by default. - Use a Browser with a Privacy-Focused Extension: If you wish to stick with a browser you are with, such as Chrome or Firefox, you can use a privacy-focused extension. For Chrome, you can use an extension like Privacy Badger, and for Firefox, you can use an extension like uBlock Origin. These extensions can help to block tracking and potentially block IP address leakage. - Regularly Update Your Software: Keeping your operating system, browser, and extensions up-to-date can help to patch any security vulnerabilities and prevent potential IP address leakage. - Be Cautious of the Websites You Visit: Be of the websites you visit, and the information you share. texts.full_text: couple thoughts. Origins Matter As I noted in my post, I actually expected the Cerner roll-out on Vancouver Island to go fine. Proven software, specialized company, no shortage of cash. But it didn’t go fine. Among the complaints were things you’d expect to have been ironed out of a mature product ages ago: data entry efficiency, user-friendly displays of relevant data at relevant places, plain old system speed and responsiveness. Why would a system rolled out in multiple US hospitals display these drawbacks? I’m wondering if some of the issue might be the origin story: Cerner is an EHR developed in the US, and the US healthcare system is quite different from the Canadian system. The US system is more complex, with multiple payers, and the EHR systems there are designed to accommodate that complexity. In Canada, the healthcare system is simpler, with a single payer, and the EHR systems need to reflect that. It's possible that the US-centric design of the Cerner system led to some of the issues encountered in Canada. The second thought is that the failure of the Cerner roll-out highlights the importance of user-centered design in healthcare IT. The medical staff in Nanaimo rejected the system because it didn't meet their needs. They wanted a system that was efficient to use, that displayed the right data in the right place, and that was responsive. The system they got didn't meet those needs, and that's a failure of the design process. In the future, it's crucial that healthcare IT systems are desig

By Buzz Lamb It’s no secret that the past two years have been trying for the global hospitality industry, but there are signs of recovery underfoot, and the arrival of 2022 brings the promise of new restaurant openings including the much anticipated The Gem in Bolton Landing on June 21st. “People have been stopping in every day for the past few weeks asking when we will open,” Paty Boccato said. The team behind this venture is Kristan Keck Farmer and his husband Kirby Farmer both of whom hail from Lake George area as well as Paty's sister-in-law Richard with 20 years in the industry. The new restaurant will bring a modern farm-to-table experience, featuring rustic chic decor that pays homage to its Adirondack roots. "The Gem is special because it not only embraces our heritage and history but also looks towards what we're capable of becoming," Kristan shared about their vision for this establishement which offers an inviting ambiance perfect for any occasion. They plan on serving a mix of American, Italian comfort foods along with fresh seafood dishes sourced from local providers. As the restaurant officially opened its doors to customers last week-end they reported that it's been "a whirlwind" since then as locals and tourists alike have rushed in wanting an early taste test what The Gem has prepared for them. They are excited about this new chapter of their family business, which is destined bring a brighter future not only on Lake George but the entire region. texts.full_text: and quantification. Two approaches are widely used in the literature to fuse multiple modalities in the segmentation networks: early-fusion (which stacks multiple modalities as different input channels) and late-fusion (which fuses the segmentation results from different modalities at the very end). These fusion methods easily suffer from the cross-modal interference caused by the input modalities which have wide variations. To address the problem, we propose a novel deep learning architecture, namely OctopusNet, to better leverage and fuse the information contained in multi-modalities. The proposed framework employs a separate encoder for each modality for feature extraction and exploits a hyper-fusion decoder to fuse the extracted features while avoiding feature explosion. The MBQS is specifically designed for this purpose, allowing researchers and clinicians alike an opportunity not only observe but quantify maternal behaviors in response to various scenarios presented during the Strange Situation Procedure. texts.full_text: I hope) well of your own compassion. I'm not saying that this will necessarily make the world a better place but we can never go back to that degree of barbarism and even in writing that, images started to pile up in my not too informed brain, images after the Second World War. What about Rwanda, Croatia, Iraq and all the Pol Pots in the world? We are still a barbarous species but it takes a refined barbarism, if there can be such a thing, to create a building that is specifically designed – let this sink in – specifically designed to herd hundreds of people into at once, in which to drop crystals giving off deadly Zyklon B gas and at which to close the doors and wait for the screaming to stop. This was Auschwitz, the death camp that was not a fleet of green vans designed to let in its own exhaust to kill those inside as was the extermination method in Treblinka. Auschwitz's was a human-designed, human-constructed, human-maintained building to kill humans. A weapon is one thing. This was pre-meditation on an enormous scale and it still makes me chill at its level of practical ingenuity. To play devil's advocate for a few paragraphs (as if we need more devils in the world), Shoah is a movie that visually is made up of talking heads (I'd say about 60% of the running time) and the rest slow movements through the locations being discussed. I can understand why this facet of the work may be hard to take. I didn't find the film flagged at all because Lanzmann is dealing with a subject so compelling that one needs the time to think, to absorb what the implications of some of the statements and how to interpret them. This week in the Rwandan capital, Kigali, nations are meeting to hammer out a plan to phase out Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) which are guilty of accelerating climate chanThis week in the Rwandan capital, Kigali, nations are meeting to hammer out a plan to phase out Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) which are guilty of accelerating climate change. HFCs, a potent greenhouse gas, are commonly used in air conditioning, refrigeration, and applications. The meeting, a significant milestone in climate change mitigation, aims to agree on a timeline and a framework to reduce HFCs, which are a significant contributor to global warming. The phasedown of HFCs, as proposed in the Kigali Amendment to the Montreal Protocol, is expected to prevent a global temperature rise of as much as 0.4°C by the end of the century, Making a significant impact in the global fight against climate change.

texts.full_text: the result of Matt Hopwood's journey on foot across Britain, collecting stories that weave together themes of love, connection, and compassion from strangers who offered him shelter along the way. His path took him hundreds of miles from Lindisfarne to Callanish in Scotland. This book captures these profound narratives, which have inspired further projects for Matt as he continues his travels around the UK gathering more stories that highlight human kindness and shared experiences. A Human Love Story is the result of a journey Matt undertook, on foot, with just a small bag and a walking stick, walking many hundreds of miles, from Lindisfarne to Callanish in the Outer Hebrides. He relied entirely on the generosity of strangers for shelter and as he went, he asked people to tell him their transforming stories….. it is these profound, honest and moving stories of love, weaving a web of tenderness, connection, and compassion, which are gathered in the book.
